detailed_spec: here's what I need: a middleware that intercepts all POST requests, validates the JSON body against a Zod schema, returns 422 with field-level errors if invalid, and passes the typed body to the handler

the component should accept a 'variant' prop with values 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'ghost', default to 'primary', and each variant needs distinct hover and active states matching the design tokens in theme.ts

create a migration that adds a 'status' enum column to the orders table with values 'pending', 'processing', 'shipped', 'delivered', 'cancelled'. Default to 'pending'. Add an index on (user_id, status) for the dashboard query.

the retry logic should: attempt 3 times, use exponential backoff starting at 100ms with jitter, only retry on 5xx and network errors, abort on 4xx, and log each attempt with the error and attempt number

build a debounced search input that waits 300ms after the user stops typing, cancels in-flight requests when a new one starts, shows a loading spinner during the fetch, and displays 'No results' when the array is empty

I need a rate limiter using the sliding window algorithm. Configuration: 100 requests per minute per API key. Store counters in Redis with MULTI/EXEC for atomicity. Return X-RateLimit-Remaining and Retry-After headers.

the form has 5 fields: name (required, 2-50 chars), email (required, valid format), password (required, 8+ chars, one uppercase, one number), confirm password (must match), and terms checkbox (must be checked). Show inline errors on blur.

implement a file upload endpoint that accepts multipart/form-data, validates file type (only .png, .jpg, .pdf), max size 10MB, streams to S3 with a UUID filename, returns the CDN URL, and cleans up on failure
contextual_request: the users table currently has columns id, email, name, created_at. We need to add a 'role' column. The existing admin check in middleware.ts hardcodes the admin email list — replace that with a role-based check using the new column.

we're using Next.js 14 with the app router. The current layout.tsx wraps everything in a single AuthProvider. I need to split it so that the /api routes don't go through the auth provider, but all /dashboard/* routes do.

the current flow is: user clicks 'Export' -> frontend calls GET /api/export -> server builds CSV in memory -> returns it. This breaks for large datasets (>100K rows). Switch to a streaming approach that starts sending chunks immediately.
our CI pipeline runs on GitHub Actions. The test step currently runs all 847 tests sequentially which takes 12 minutes. Split them into 4 parallel shards using the matrix strategy.

the monitoring shows that the /api/search endpoint has p99 latency of 2.3 seconds. The query joins 3 tables without proper indexes. Add the missing indexes and refactor the query to use a CTE for the user filter.
